steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank introduces a sentence about demonstration sports chosen for Games in specific host countries. The passage has already explained that demonstration sports are meant to showcase sports from the host country's culture, giving examples of korfball (Netherlands) and kyudō (Japan). The blank needs a word that signals the relationship between those facts and the upcoming examples.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Additionally,: implies the sentence merely adds a new point. It ignores the meaningful match between sport and host country. ✗
  • Choice B — By contrast,: signals opposition or a surprising difference. Nothing in the sentence contradicts what came before. ✗
  • Choice C — Fittingly,: signals that the examples are appropriate or well-matched to the stated purpose — korfball for Amsterdam, kyudō for Tokyo. ✓
  • Choice D — Nevertheless,: signals concession despite an obstacle. No obstacle is mentioned. ✗
That is the correct answer.
